name: MCP Schema Signature Verification (SchemaPin-Inspired)
description: Cryptographic schema verification rules inspired by SchemaPin — ECDSA P-256 signatures, .well-known discovery, TOFU key pinning, revocation checking, and trust bundles

patterns.description: Unsupported SchemaPin version — potential compatibility attack
Invalid public key format — not a valid PEM key
Revoked key detected in schema signature document
Signature expiration field present — check if expired
Schema version binding present — lineage chain enforcement active
Key compromise revocation — private key was compromised
Privilege withdrawn — key holder authorization revoked
Revocation endpoint present — should be checked for standalone revocations
Key fingerprint format — valid SHA-256 fingerprint detected
Trust bundle detected — offline/air-gapped verification mode
Empty developer name — suspicious schema signing metadata
Tor onion domain in schema — high-risk anonymous signing
DNS TXT cross-verification record — second-channel trust

require_signature.description: All MCP tool schemas MUST have a valid cryptographic signature

check_revocation.description: Verify key is not in revoked_keys array or standalone revocation document

verify_fingerprint.description: Calculate SHA-256 fingerprint of DER-encoded public key and match against pinned

canonicalize_schema.description: JSON schema must be canonicalized (sorted keys, no whitespace) before verification

check_expiration.description: If expires_at is present, check against current time

verify_lineage.description: If schema_version + previous_hash present, verify chain of custody

pin_key.description: On first use, pin the public key. Reject if key changes without user consent
